response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine language-tooling interest — he built a D test runner, assertion library, and worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments — but the core requirements of this role are not met. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms, with only incidental Java exposure and no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are the non-negotiable pillars of this enterprise IDE position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI plus self-built developer tooling (test runner, assertion library)
Proven track record on complex, high-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency async I/O)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, and open-source maintainership
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; primary stacks are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM
No recent work on enterprise frameworks, build tools, or application server integration

key_factors.factor: Large-scale systems and quality engineering background
